DatCol

Authors

Andrea Boyd

Katia Quintanilla

Isabel F. Escapa

Published

May 20, 2026

Overview

This repository contains all analysis code and documentation for the manuscript:

D-alanine Amino Transferase (Dat) promotes Staphylococcus aureus colonization fitness on human nasal respiratory epithelium

Repository Structure and Data Availability

All analyses are provided as individual Quarto (.qmd) notebooks, which can be rendered independently. Each notebook reads processed .rds files from the dataframes/ folder and generates publication-quality figures and statistical outputs.

To reproduce the analyses:

  • Clone or download this repository to your local machine.
  • Download the dataframes/ and Tn-Seq/ folders and place it in your working directory.
  • Download all .qmd notebooks from the repository and keep them in the same directory.
  • Install required R packages listed in R Session Info using renv or manually.
  • Render the notebooks using Quarto to generate figures and results.

The directory structure should look like this:

your_project/
├── dataframes/          
├── Tn-Seq/
└── [analysis notebooks saved as .qmd files]

Set environment variable as follows in R:

Sys.setenv(BOX_PATH = "/path/to/your_project")

Alternatively, add to your .Renviron file:

BOX_PATH=/path/to/your_project

The whole project can be rendered using RStudio’s “Render” or “Built” options or in the terminal via:

quarto render

Main Analysis Notebooks

1. Tn-Seq Pipeline

2. Tn-Seq Essential Genes Analysis

3. Competition Assays on HNO-ALI

4. Complementation Assays on HNO-ALI

5. Growth Curves (OD600)

6. Coculture Assays with Corynebacterium pseudodiphtheriticum

7. Cytotoxicity Assays (LDH)

Other Notebooks

1. Linear Mixed Model Workflow

2. R Session Info

3. References

Tn-Seq Pipeline
Source Code
# Overview {.unnumbered}

::: {.callout-note icon="false"}
## This repository contains all analysis code and documentation for the manuscript: 
D-alanine Amino Transferase (Dat) promotes Staphylococcus aureus colonization fitness on human nasal respiratory epithelium
:::

## Repository Structure and Data Availability

All analyses are provided as individual Quarto (`.qmd`) notebooks, which can be rendered independently. Each notebook reads processed `.rds` files from the `dataframes/` folder and generates publication-quality figures and statistical outputs.

To reproduce the analyses:

-   Clone or download this [repository](https://github.com/KLemonLab/XXXXXX) to your local machine.
-   Download the `dataframes/` and `Tn-Seq/` folders and place it in your working directory.
-   Download all `.qmd` notebooks from the repository and keep them in the same directory.
-   Install required R packages listed in [R Session Info](Manuscript_DatCol_RSession.html) using `renv` or manually.
-   Render the notebooks using Quarto to generate figures and results.

The directory structure should look like this:

```         
your_project/
├── dataframes/          
├── Tn-Seq/
└── [analysis notebooks saved as .qmd files]
```

Set environment variable as follows in R:

```         
Sys.setenv(BOX_PATH = "/path/to/your_project")
```

Alternatively, add to your `.Renviron` file:

```         
BOX_PATH=/path/to/your_project
```

The whole project can be rendered using RStudio's "Render" or "Built" options or in the terminal via:

```
quarto render
```

## Main Analysis Notebooks


1\. [Tn-Seq Pipeline](Manuscript_DatCol_Tn-Seq_Pipeline.html)

2\. [Tn-Seq Essential Genes Analysis](Manuscript_DatCol_Tn-Seq_Figures.html)

3\. [Competition Assays on HNO-ALI](Manuscript_DatCol_HNO_Competition.html)

4\. [Complementation Assays on HNO-ALI](Manuscript_DatCol_HNO_Complementation.html)

5\. [Growth Curves (OD600)](Manuscript_DatCol_Growth_Curves.html)

6\. [Coculture Assays with *Corynebacterium pseudodiphtheriticum*](Manuscript_DatCol_HNO_Cps.html)

7\. [Cytotoxicity Assays (LDH)](Manuscript_DatCol_LDH.html)
